justification: All rubric criteria met: (1) Both training and serving pipelines fully designed with clear components and boundaries. (2) 100ms P99 constraint explicitly addressed with latency budget breakdown (~10ms feature fetch + ~5ms inference + ~15ms overhead = ~30ms typical, 70ms headroom). (3) Feature store identified as necessary with online (Redis) and offline (Parquet) stores, explicitly called out as critical to prevent training/serving skew. (4) Model versioning via Model Registry with immutable artifacts, rollback via Promotion Gate with shadow scoring/champion-challenger pattern. (5) Team size factored — managed services recommended throughout, custom code estimated at ~2000 LOC, phased implementation for 4-person team. (6) Detailed trade_offs (5 decisions with gains/sacrifices/alternatives) and risks (6 risks with severity and mitigation) present.

observations.text: Exceptionally detailed latency budget breakdown proving the 100ms P99 is achievable with specific numbers per component
Shadow scoring pattern is a sophisticated alternative to simple A/B testing, with clear rationale for why it's better for a small team
Circuit breaker with heuristic fallback ensures checkout is never blocked by ML infrastructure failure — addresses production reliability beyond the rubric requirements
Feature schema hash validation to detect training/serving skew is a strong operational detail
Practical implementation guidance with phased approach and LOC estimates shows team-size awareness
Explicit warning against Pandas in serving path with quantified overhead (20-50ms) demonstrates deep practical knowledge
Could have been more explicit about model drift risk from weekly retraining cadence — mentioned as a flexibility point but not called out as a primary risk

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Architecture for a real-time ML delivery time estimation service on the checkout critical path. The design separates the system into four bounded contexts: Feature Engineering, Model Training Pipeline, Model Serving, and Monitoring/Rollback. The serving layer uses pre-loaded models with a lightweight inference path to hit <100ms P99. A shadow-scoring pattern enables safe weekly model promotion with automatic rollback.
